text.title: ar-design-patterns Failure Modes
text.summary: Migrated antipatterns for ar-design-patterns as failure modes. Add detector + repair clauses where missing.

failure-mode.description: Applying ARKit-spec patterns directly to WebAR without checking browser support.
failure-mode.detector: TBD — observable signal that this antipattern is happening
failure-mode.repair: iOS Safari does not support WebXR Device API; AR on iPhone must use AR Quick Look (model-viewer) or a native wrapper. WebAR has the worst tracking parity across platforms.

failure-mode.description: Hiding the "Exit AR" button once the AR experience starts.
failure-mode.detector: TBD — observable signal that this antipattern is happening
failure-mode.repair: Trapping users in AR is both a safety issue and an accessibility failure. Exit must always be one tap away.

failure-mode.description: LLMs default to "place a 3D model on the floor" without specifying surface size or fallback when scanning fails.
failure-mode.detector: TBD — observable signal that this antipattern is happening
failure-mode.repair: Require agents to explicitly specify anchor type, fallback chain, and tracking timeout.

failure-mode.description: Agents conflate ARKit and ARCore feature sets.
failure-mode.detector: TBD — observable signal that this antipattern is happening
failure-mode.repair: Image tracking, occlusion, and anchors differ; pin platform in the prompt.

failure-mode.description: WebAR generated samples often skip the iOS-Safari constraints.
failure-mode.detector: TBD — observable signal that this antipattern is happening
failure-mode.repair: Must use Quick Look on iPhone, not WebXR. Require browser compatibility check.

failure-mode.description: Agents under-spec lighting requirements — say "good lighting" instead of providing a lux floor or runtime check.
failure-mode.detector: TBD — observable signal that this antipattern is happening
failure-mode.repair: Require minimum lux and runtime feedback for lighting conditions.

failure-mode.description: Voice-command coverage is forgotten unless explicitly required.
failure-mode.detector: TBD — observable signal that this antipattern is happening
failure-mode.repair: Force agent to list voice equivalents for every interaction.

failure-mode.description: Privacy of camera + spatial-mesh data is rarely flagged.
failure-mode.detector: TBD — observable signal that this antipattern is happening
failure-mode.repair: Require a privacy note in any shared-anchor design.

failure-mode.description: Agents propose smooth dolly-zoom transitions that cause AR motion sickness.
failure-mode.detector: TBD — observable signal that this antipattern is happening
failure-mode.repair: Clamp camera-driven motion. Require motion-sensitivity tester review.

failure-mode.detector: TBD — observable signal that this antipattern is happening
failure-mode.repair: Require: outdoor real-environment test, low-light test, motion-sensitive tester, accessibility partner review, privacy review for any cloud-anchor / world-mesh upload.
